game.next_round: Western Conference Finals vs Oklahoma City

source_notes.official_book: NBA official scorer report supplied by user: 20260515_SASMIN_book.pdf
source_notes.recap: AP recap supplied by user: Stephon Castle gives Spurs 32 points in a 139-109 blowout to eliminate Timberwolves in Game 6.

market_positions.selection: Yes - San Antonio wins by over 13.5 points

turning_points: San Antonio controlled the opening quarter 36-27 behind Stephon Castle's immediate shot-making and Fox pace control.
The Spurs pushed the lead as high as 29 in the second quarter before Minnesota cut it to 13 by halftime.
San Antonio erased the Minnesota comeback threat with a 36-23 third quarter, entering the fourth up 110-84.
Minnesota never led and never forced a late leverage state, validating the live spread blowout-continuation angle.
Randle reached 7 rebounds despite only 23:42 minutes, cashing the 7+ rebound position by the exact threshold.

Stephon Castle.model_relevance: Primary volatility breaker. Castle's early 3PT burst created immediate spread pressure and forced Minnesota into chase mode.

Victor Wembanyama.model_relevance: Scoring volume was secondary, but rim deterrence and vertical gravity drove Minnesota efficiency suppression.

De'Aaron Fox.model_relevance: Pace control and transition ignition validated Spurs backcourt dominance tag.

Julian Champagnie.model_relevance: Spacing wing ceiling materially widened San Antonio margin and supports bench/role-player shooting volatility boost.

Dylan Harper.model_relevance: Bench scoring stability confirmed San Antonio depth advantage.

Anthony Edwards.model_relevance: High volume but inefficient; Spurs switch-heavy defense forced tough shot diet and limited playmaking conversion.

Julius Randle.model_relevance: Rebound ladder cashed at 7, but offensive role collapsed. Strong example of rebound-positive / scoring-negative split in blowout state.

Naz Reid.model_relevance: Bench scoring kept Minnesota from total collapse but did not affect spread outcome.

Terrence Shannon Jr..model_relevance: Garbage-time and second-unit scoring preserved Minnesota team total range but not competitiveness.

betting_result_summary.notes: Clean two-hit card: SAS -13.5 live spread hit by 16.5 points of cover cushion, and Julius Randle 7+ rebounds cashed exactly at threshold.

why_it_won: San Antonio led by 13 at halftime and won the second half by 17.
Spurs shot 55.7% FG and 47.4% from three.
Minnesota shot 37.7% FG on 106 attempts and never created efficient halfcourt flow.
San Antonio posted 34 assists and 60 rebounds, creating both efficiency and possession advantage.
Randle reached 6 rebounds by halftime, giving the ladder a strong early path.
Minnesota's poor shooting generated rebound volume despite Randle's scoring collapse.
He finished exactly at 7 rebounds, making this a hook/threshold win rather than a high-cushion prop.

san_antonio: Upgrade backcourt creation and shooting variance ceiling into WCF baseline.
Maintain Wembanyama defensive deterrence as a team efficiency suppressor even when his scoring volume is not ceiling-level.
Depth scoring should remain active with Harper, Champagnie, Kornet, and Bryant creating support lanes.
Blowout continuation logic validated when Spurs lead by double digits with shooting efficiency and defensive rim control intact.
minnesota: Flag elimination-game offensive collapse risk as recurring pattern.
Downgrade Randle scoring reliability against length/switch-heavy defenses while preserving rebound path in high-miss environments.
Edwards volume can remain high while efficiency and assists collapse under elite pressure.
Gobert offensive/rebounding floor is fragile against Wembanyama/Kornet size layers.

patch_recommendations: Add CASTLE_PLAYOFF_CEILING_SPIKE to Spurs wing/backcourt module.
Add MIN_ELIMINATION_NO_SHOW_RISK for future playoff elimination reads.
Separate Randle rebound projection from Randle points projection; poor shooting did not prevent rebound cashing.
Treat Spurs 3PT volume plus Wembanyama rim deterrence as a spread-expansion combo.
Apply BLOWOUT_CONTINUATION_VALIDATED when favorite leads 10+ at half, owns the glass, and opponent eFG is suppressed.
